issues.current_text: a new 3-dimensional array with dimensions `n1 x n2 x n3`, with all elements initialized to their default value
issues.problem: Slightly inconsistent with the 2D version, which explicitly states the type `Array[Array[T]]`. The 3D/4D/5D return descriptions omit the explicit nested array type.
issues.suggestion: a new `Array[Array[Array[T]]]` of size `n1 x n2 x n3`, with all elements initialized to their default value

issues.current_text: a new 4-dimensional array with dimensions `n1 x n2 x n3 x n4`, with all elements initialized to their default value
issues.problem: Same inconsistency: 2D version uses explicit `Array[Array[T]]` form while higher dimensions describe shape in prose.
issues.suggestion: a new `Array[Array[Array[Array[T]]]]` of size `n1 x n2 x n3 x n4`, with all elements initialized to their default value

issues.current_text: a new 5-dimensional array with dimensions `n1 x n2 x n3 x n4 x n5`, with all elements initialized to their default value
issues.suggestion: a new `Array[Array[Array[Array[Array[T]]]]]` of size `n1 x n2 x n3 x n4 x n5`, with all elements initialized to their default value

issues.current_text: a 3-dimensional array of size `n1 x n2 x n3`, where each element is the result of computing `elem`
issues.problem: Does not mention that `elem` is re-evaluated for every element (a key behavior emphasized in the 1D `fill` doc comment). Same observation applies to the 2D, 4D, and 5D `fill` return descriptions.
issues.suggestion: a 3-dimensional array of size `n1 x n2 x n3`, where each element contains the result of (separately) computing `elem`

general_feedback: The added @param, @tparam, and @return descriptions are accurate, clear, and consistent with Scaladoc conventions (lowercase start, backticked code references, concise phrasing). They correctly describe the runtime behavior and types. Minor suggestions concern stylistic consistency across the dimensional families (ofDim/fill/tabulate) — particularly whether to spell out the nested `Array[Array[...]]` type and whether to note that `fill`'s `elem` is re-evaluated per element. None of these are errors; the documentation is good as-is.
